raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) technical content and claim–evidence assessment (conceptual framing, logic, novelty) or (B) primarily language/style and minor structural edits, leaving technical claims largely untouched?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "A review should assess whether the section’s central contribution and thesis are clearly and coherently framed above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "The argument from benchmark motivation to design to comparative conclusions needs checking for internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about scores, violation rates, and takeaways must align with the described tasks and scoring rubric as reported.",
    "R1.4": "The review should gauge the stated novelty of the benchmark and controller advantages relative to nearest prior work, without auditing scholarship coverage.",
    "R1.6": "Improving sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone is a standard part of reviewing a section.",
    "RX.1": "Feedback should note uncertainties and limitations (e.g., error bars, seeds, task scope) to avoid overstating confidence.",
    "RX.2": "The review must guard against overgeneralized conclusions that exceed what the presented evidence supports.",
    "RX.3": "The review should stay within the scope of evaluating this section rather than redesigning experiments or expanding the paper.",
    "RX.4": "Comments should avoid encouraging unsafe real-world deployment or misuse of methods beyond benchmarked contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Any critique should be traceable to specific sentences, figures, or numbers in the provided section."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) technical content and claim–evidence assessment (conceptual framing, logic, novelty) or (B) primarily language/style and minor structural edits, leaving technical claims largely untouched?

rationale.R1.1: A review should assess whether the section’s central contribution and thesis are clearly and coherently framed above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The argument from benchmark motivation to design to comparative conclusions needs checking for internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about scores, violation rates, and takeaways must align with the described tasks and scoring rubric as reported.
rationale.R1.4: The review should gauge the stated novelty of the benchmark and controller advantages relative to nearest prior work, without auditing scholarship coverage.
rationale.R1.6: Improving sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, and tone is a standard part of reviewing a section.
rationale.RX.1: Feedback should note uncertainties and limitations (e.g., error bars, seeds, task scope) to avoid overstating confidence.
rationale.RX.2: The review must guard against overgeneralized conclusions that exceed what the presented evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: The review should stay within the scope of evaluating this section rather than redesigning experiments or expanding the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Comments should avoid encouraging unsafe real-world deployment or misuse of methods beyond benchmarked contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Any critique should be traceable to specific sentences, figures, or numbers in the provided section.
